title: Nobody in Missouri Claims This 230-Pound Tortoise
script: Tortoise on the loose! Columbia, Missouri animal control picked up a large African spurred tortoise wandering near the intersection of Route Z and Enterprise Drive at 12:30 in the afternoon — just strolling through like he had somewhere to be. Male, appeared healthy, no ID. Officers posted photos to social media. Nobody claimed him. Here's the thing about African spurred tortoises: they're the third-largest tortoise species on Earth, they can weigh up to 230 pounds, and — this is key — they can dig under fenced yards and just... leave. Nobody sees them go. Animal control kept him on a 5-day stray hold. If no one steps forward, he goes to the Central Missouri Humane Society. He's been living in Columbia for at least a day. So whose 230-pound tortoise is just wandering around Missouri?
